Definition: the order of x modulo N is the smallest positive integer r such
The problem of finding the order is a di�cult problem for a classical computer:
no classical polynomial algorithm is known. By this we mean an algorithm poly-
nomial in the number L of bits necessary to specify the problem, for example the
number of bits necessary for the binary expression of N . Thus L satisfies
Exercise: find the order of 5 modulo 21
Theorem: r always exists (provided x and N are coprime) and
The quantum algorithm for order finding is simply the algorithm for phase estima-
tion applied to a unitary operator
U _ {x, N} | y \rangle \equiv | x y \bmod N \rangle \tag {22.1}
where y is a L-bit integer, thus
acts on a vector space of
\left| u _ {s} \right\rangle \equiv \frac {1}{\sqrt {r}} \sum_ {k = 0} ^ {r - 1} \exp \left[ \frac {- 2 \pi i s k}{r} \right] \left| x ^ {k} \bmod N \right\rangle \tag {22.2}
U _ {x, N} | u _ {s} \rangle = \exp \left[ \frac {2 \pi i s}{r} \right] | u _ {s} \rangle \tag {22.3}
Applying now the phase estimation algorithm gives an estimation the phase
we can deduce r (via the continued
fraction algorithm, see later), i.e. the order of x modulo N .

The quantum algorithm for order finding is simply the algorithm for phase estima-
tion applied to a unitary operator
U _ {x, N} | y \rangle \equiv | x y \bmod N \rangle \tag {22.1}
where y is a L-bit integer, thus
acts on a vector space of
\left| u _ {s} \right\rangle \equiv \frac {1}{\sqrt {r}} \sum_ {k = 0} ^ {r - 1} \exp \left[ \frac {- 2 \pi i s k}{r} \right] \left| x ^ {k} \bmod N \right\rangle \tag {22.2}
U _ {x, N} | u _ {s} \rangle = \exp \left[ \frac {2 \pi i s}{r} \right] | u _ {s} \rangle \tag {22.3}
Applying now the phase estimation algorithm gives an estimation the phase
we can deduce r (via the continued
fraction algorithm, see later), i.e. the order of x modulo N .

\begin{array}{l} U _ {x, N} | u _ {s} \rangle = \frac {1}{\sqrt {r}} \sum_ {k = 0} ^ {r - 1} \exp \left[ \frac {- 2 \pi i s k}{r} \right] | x ^ {k + 1} \bmod N \rangle \\ = \frac {1}{\sqrt {r}} \sum_ {k = 1} ^ {r} \exp \left[ \frac {- 2 \pi i s (k - 1)}{r} \right] | x ^ {k} \bmod {\mathrm{N}} \rangle \\ = \frac {1}{\sqrt {r}} \sum_ {k = 0} ^ {r - 1} \exp \left[ \frac {- 2 \pi i s (k - 1)}{r} \right] | x ^ {k} \bmod \mathrm{N} \rangle \\ = \exp \left[ \frac {2 \pi i s}{r} \right] \frac {1}{\sqrt {r}} \sum_ {k = 0} ^ {r - 1} \exp \left[ \frac {- 2 \pi i s k}{r} \right] | x ^ {k} \bmod N \rangle \\ = \exp \left[ \frac {2 \pi i s}{r} \right] | u _ {s} \rangle \tag {22.4} \\ \end{array}
